Tests if multiplying `Number.NaN` by `-0` results in `NaN` using an assertion.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the behavior of the multiplication operation involving `Number.NaN` and `-0` in JavaScript. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the result of multiplying `Number.NaN` by `-0` is `NaN`, and that the `isNaN` function correctly identifies this result as `true`. This test ensures that the JavaScript engine adheres to the expected behavior of the IEEE 754 standard for floating-point arithmetic, specifically in handling operations with `NaN`. The code is part of a test suite to validate the correctness of mathematical operations in JavaScript.
